# ResumeJP (resumejp.com)

> ResumeJP is a free online Japanese resume builder at https://www.resumejp.com. It creates authentic Japanese resumes — 履歴書 (rirekisho) and 職務経歴書 (shokumukeirekisho) — from JIS-standard, MHLW (厚生労働省) government, new-graduate, mid-career, part-time/arubaito, dispatch, and English CV templates. Users type in any language (English, Japanese, Nepali and more), convert the text to natural business Japanese with one click, and download a print-ready A4 or A3 PDF. No account, no signup, completely free; all resume data stays in the user's browser and is never stored on a server.

Brand: "ResumeJP", also written "resume jp", "resumejp", or "resumejp.com". Not affiliated with japaneseresume.com, nihonresume.com, or resume.ne.jp.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.resumejp.com/): Overview of the Japanese resume builder, all features, and FAQ.
- [Resume Editor](https://www.resumejp.com/editor): The free step-by-step rirekisho editor with live preview and PDF download.
- [Japanese Resume Templates](https://www.resumejp.com/templates): Free 履歴書 templates — JIS A3/A4, MHLW, modern, new-grad, part-time, dispatch, and English CV — with guidance on which to choose.
- [Part-Time (Arubaito) Resume](https://www.resumejp.com/arubaito-resume): Make a part-time/アルバイト rirekisho — residence-status fields, weekly shift-availability grid, Japanese-level field, and ready-to-edit 志望動機 examples by job type (convenience store, restaurant, cafe, factory, hotel, delivery, moving, retail).
- [Blog](https://www.resumejp.com/blog): Practical, foreigner-aware guides for Japanese resumes and part-time jobs.
- [Tools](https://www.resumejp.com/tools): Free browser tools — 和暦/西暦 era converter, 満年齢 age calculator, 30×40 mm rirekisho photo cropper, and 自己PR character counter.
- [How to Write a Japanese Resume](https://www.resumejp.com/guide/how-to-write-a-japanese-resume): Step-by-step 履歴書 writing guide — photo rules, date/era formats, 学歴・職歴 phrasing, licenses, self-PR, and common mistakes.
- [Rirekisho vs Shokumukeirekisho](https://www.resumejp.com/guide/rirekisho-vs-shokumukeirekisho): The difference between Japan's two job-application documents and when each is required.
- [The 28-Hour Rule for Students](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/28-hour-rule-students-work-limit): What the student-visa 28-hour weekly work limit (資格外活動許可) means and how to show it on a resume.
- [Rirekisho Photo Rules](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/rirekisho-photo-rules): The 30×40 mm photo rules and how to take an acceptable one with a phone.
- [Handwritten vs Printed Rirekisho](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/handwritten-vs-printed-rirekisho): Which employers prefer for part-time jobs, and how to do each well.
- [Filling In the 本人希望記入欄](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/honnin-kibo-kinyuran-part-time): The applicant's-request field — the safe default phrase and the salary mistake to avoid.
- [Phone Script After Applying](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/phone-script-after-applying-baito): A polite Japanese phone script for calling a store after applying to an arubaito.
- [Writing 自己PR (Self-PR)](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/how-to-write-jiko-pr-self-pr): The claim-proof-relevance structure for the self-PR box, with editable examples.
- [Writing 学歴・職歴](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/gakureki-shokureki-education-work-history): Education and work-history conventions, including overseas schools and jobs.
- [JLPT Levels Explained](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/jlpt-levels-which-do-you-need): What N1–N5 mean and which Japanese level different jobs expect.
- [Residence Status on Your Resume](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/zairyu-shikaku-residence-status-resume): The common 在留資格 types and exactly how to show them on a rirekisho.
- [Printing at a Convenience Store](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/print-rirekisho-convenience-store): Printing your resume PDF at 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, or Lawson.
- [令和 vs Western Dates](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/japanese-era-dates-reiwa-resume): The Japanese era calendar and keeping dates consistent on a resume.
- [Emailing Your Rirekisho](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/email-rirekisho-japanese-etiquette): Japanese email etiquette and a copy-paste application-email template.
- [Part-Time Interview Questions](https://www.resumejp.com/blog/part-time-interview-questions-baito): The questions arubaito managers ask and honest ways to answer them.
- [About](https://www.resumejp.com/about): Who builds ResumeJP (the DIGI スタート! studio), the mission, and how the free tool is supported.
- [Contact](https://www.resumejp.com/contact): Reach the team by email (digistartnp@gmail.com) for resume questions, corrections, features, or partnerships.
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.resumejp.com/privacy-policy): Data handling — resume data stays in the browser's local storage.
- [Terms of Service](https://www.resumejp.com/terms): Acceptable use, intellectual property, advertising, and limitation of liability.
- [Disclaimer](https://www.resumejp.com/disclaimer): General information only — not legal, immigration, or employment advice.

## Key facts

- Cost: free, no account or email required.
- Output: print-ready PDF at exact A4/A3 size; also XeLaTeX source and JSON backup.
- Languages: fill in English, Japanese, Nepali and others; one-click conversion to business Japanese.
- Templates: JIS standard (A3 and A4), MHLW 2021 government format, modern/tech, new-graduate, mid-career, part-time (arubaito), dispatch registration, English CV.
- Photo: auto-resized to the standard 30×40 mm rirekisho photo cell.
- Part-time/arubaito focus: residence-status (在留資格) fields for foreign applicants, weekly shift-availability grid, Japanese-level (JLPT) field, and pre-written 志望動機 (motivation statement) examples by job type.
- Student work limit: holders of a 資格外活動許可 may work up to 28 hours/week (40 during official school vacations).
- Privacy: resume data is stored only in the browser (localStorage), never on a server.
